record.field: Docmost is an open-source collaborative wiki and documentation platform, a self-hosted alternative to Confluence and Notion.

Docmost is an open-source collaborative wiki and documentation platform, a self-hosted alternative to Confluence and Notion.

It lets teams create, organize and co-author knowledge bases, internal documentation and project wikis in a real-time rich-text editor. Content is structured into spaces with granular permissions and user groups, while page history and inline comments keep collaboration auditable. Built on a modern TypeScript stack with PostgreSQL and Redis, it deploys via Docker and can run fully self-contained, even in air-gapped environments.

Key features
  • Real-time collaborative rich-text editor with tables, LaTeX math and callouts
  • Spaces, nested page hierarchy and granular view/edit permissions with user groups
  • Inline comments, full page version history and one-click restore
  • Built-in diagrams via Draw.io, Excalidraw and Mermaid
  • Workspace-wide search, file attachments and embeds
  • Markdown and HTML import/export, with multi-language UI
Best for

Teams and organizations that want a self-hosted, data-sovereign knowledge base or internal wiki without per-seat SaaS lock-in, including privacy-sensitive or air-gapped deployments.

services: docmost: image: docmost/docmost:0.4.1 depends_on: - db - redis environment: - APP_URL - APP_SECRET - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/${POSTGRES_DB}?schema=public - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./docmost:/app/data/storage container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:16-alpine environment: - POSTGRES_DB - POSTGRES_USER - POSTGRES_PASSWORD restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./db_docmost_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:7.2-alpine restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./redis_docmost_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # Si POSTGRES_USER/POSTGRES_DB vienen vacíos (la imagen usa 'postgres' por defecto), # caemos a 'postgres': con $POSTGRES_USER vacío pg_dump intentaría el rol "root" del SO # y fallaría con «role "root" does not exist». docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}}" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura (mismo default a 'postgres') docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}}" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres} -d \${POSTGRES_DB:-postgres} -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
